evals.prompt: Add this repo's docs to Browzer so I can semantic-search them.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-documents and runs `browzer workspace docs` to open the picker.
expectations: The embed-documents skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace docs`
Agent did NOT run `browzer init` or `browzer workspace index` instead

evals.prompt: I'm out of chunk quota in Browzer. Free some up by removing docs I don't need.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-documents, explains unchecking indexed docs deletes them, then runs the picker.
expectations: The embed-documents skill was invoked
Agent warned that unchecking = delete
Agent ran `browzer workspace docs`

evals.prompt: Index folders and symbols for this repo.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to embed-workspace-graphs, NOT embed-documents.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked, NOT embed-documents

evals.prompt: Our team just shipped v2 of the internal auth playbook as a 60-page PDF that landed in docs/runbooks/auth-v2.pdf. It replaces docs/runbooks/auth-v1.md, which should no longer show up in any search results. I need v2 searchable by EOD alongside the existing markdown runbooks. Walk me through the exact steps, including how to make v1 drop out of the index.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-documents, runs the picker, confirms PDFs are supported, explains that unchecking auth-v1.md deletes it from the index, and verifies the final state.
expectations: The embed-documents skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace docs`
Agent confirmed PDFs are a supported format
Agent explained that unchecking auth-v1.md in the picker will delete it from the index
Agent suggested a post-check via `browzer search` to confirm v1 no longer surfaces

evals.prompt: Find the section of our on-call runbook that explains the PagerDuty escalation policy for staging incidents. The runbook is already indexed.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to semantic-search, NOT embed-documents (the doc is already embedded — this is a retrieval task).
expectations: The semantic-search skill was invoked, NOT embed-documents
Agent ran `browzer search` with a query about PagerDuty escalation for staging
Agent did NOT run `browzer workspace docs`

evals.prompt: I just rewrote docs/architecture.md to reflect our switch from SQS to BullMQ. Only that file changed. Refresh it in the workspace index without re-processing the other 40 docs we've already embedded.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-documents, opens the picker, explains that re-selecting the single changed file triggers a re-embed for just that file.
expectations: The embed-documents skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace docs`
Agent explained that re-selecting the single file in the picker re-embeds only that file
Agent did NOT claim a single-file flag exists

evals.prompt: I'm indexing the Langfuse v4 Python SDK docs (about 200 .md files under third-party/langfuse/) plus 8 PDFs of incident postmortems I dropped in docs/postmortems/. I'm worried the 200 Langfuse files will blow past our chunk quota before the postmortems get in. Make sure both formats end up in the index and give me a strategy for keeping the quota under control if we do run out.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-documents, runs the picker, and outlines a quota-aware selection strategy (prioritize postmortems, possibly deselect non-essential Langfuse sections).
expectations: The embed-documents skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace docs`
Agent confirmed both markdown and PDF are supported in a single picker run
Agent proposed a quota-aware strategy (e.g. deselecting less-critical Langfuse subpaths) if the quota is tight
Agent did NOT loop over files via shell scripts or assume a non-existent batch flag
